import React from 'react'; function Thirteen() { return (

Focus Group Script for Our Language-Learning Social Network

04-27-2026

What's The Purpose of this Focus Group?

Hi Spanish 102 students. The purpose of this project is to create a social network that allows students who are learning foreign languages to effectively study and review. It creates a more interactive mode of communication, meant for educational purposes. Today you'll be testing out our site.


Login and Test Our Network

  1. Onboarding walkthrough (3 min) — We'll ask each tester to open the app and create a profile as if they were genuinely signing up. No instructions beyond "make an account." We want to see where people get stuck and where things click naturally.
  2. Core feature exploration (4 min) — Testers will browse the feed, find another user who speaks a language they're learning, and attempt to initiate some kind of connection — whether that's a message, a match, or a follow. Again, we stay quiet and observe.
  3. Structured task: Post something (3 min) — We'll ask testers to post something in the language they're learning. It can be one word, a sentence, whatever feels natural. The goal is to see whether the posting flow feels encouraging or intimidating, and whether they'd actually want to share something that imperfect with strangers.
  4. Open reaction (2–3 min) — Testers get to say whatever they want. What did they notice? What annoyed them? What surprised them? We take notes and don't push back.

Questions to Think About

After the session steps, we'll move into a short conversation. These are the questions we want to get at:

  • What made you want to learn another language in the first place? Was there a moment, a person, a place?
  • Have you ever tried to practice with a real person — a tutor, a language exchange partner, anyone? How did that go?
  • What does a good interaction with someone who speaks your target language actually look like to you? Is it about correction, or conversation, or something else entirely?
  • What would make you feel safe enough to post something imperfect — a bad sentence, a wrong conjugation — in front of strangers?
  • If this app existed exactly the way you'd want it to, what would keep you coming back to it every week?
  • What's one thing you've seen in another social app — any app — that you'd want to steal for this?
  • Is there anything about the way we've designed this that feels wrong for the kind of community you'd actually want to be part of?
); } export default Thirteen;
